channel.title: CyberNetSec - Regulatory
channel.description: Latest regulatory threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: HealthNet Insurance Breach Exposes Sensitive Health Data of 11 Million Patients via Misconfigured AWS S3 Bucket
item.description:
HealthNet Insurance, a major US health provider, has disclosed a data breach that exposed the personal and medical information of 11 million individuals. The breach, discovered on June 15, 2026, originated from a misconfigured Amazon Web Services (AWS) S3 bucket that contained access keys to other s...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Cloud Security, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: EU Unveils 'CYBER-AI Act' with Strict Security Mandates and Heavy Fines for High-Risk AI
item.description:
The European Commission has introduced the 'Cybersecurity of Artificial Intelligence Act' (CYBER-AI Act), a new regulation that imposes stringent cybersecurity requirements on high-risk AI systems. Working alongside the existing AI Act, this legislation mandates secure-by-design principles, threat m...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: EDPB Adopts Common Template for GDPR Data Breach Notifications
item.description:
The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) has adopted a common, standardized template for organizations to use when notifying supervisory authorities of a personal data breach under the GDPR. This initiative aims to simplify the compliance process and harmonize the currently fragmented reporting req...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited PTC and Cisco Flaws to KEV Catalog, Mandates Federal Patching
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added two new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, confirming they are under active exploitation. The flaws are CVE-2026-12569, an improper input validation bug in PTC's Windchill and FlexPLM produc...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: FCC Adopts New Cybersecurity Rules to Safeguard Emergency Alert System (EAS)
item.description:
On June 25, 2026, the U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) adopted new rules requiring operators of the Emergency Alert System (EAS) to implement specific cybersecurity measures to protect the critical public warning infrastructure. The rules mandate actions like installing firewalls, monito...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: Healthcare Data Breaches at MN Epilepsy Group, Campbell University, and City of Middletown Expose Patient Info
item.description:
Several U.S. organizations have recently disclosed data breaches affecting sensitive patient information. Minnesota Epilepsy Group reported a breach impacting patients' names, Social Security numbers, and medical data after a network intrusion in March-April 2026. The City of Middletown, NY, notifie...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Ransomware, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Black's Insurance and Financial Services Discloses Data Breach Affecting SSNs
item.description:
CG Black Financial Services, operating as Black's Insurance and Financial Services, has reported a data breach that may have compromised sensitive personal information, including Social Security numbers. A notification was filed with the Vermont Attorney General's Office on June 24, 2026. At present...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Data Breach, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Shifts Strategy to Protect 'Crown Jewels' of Critical Infrastructure
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) is shifting its strategy from a broad, sector-based approach to one that focuses on identifying and protecting the specific assets and systems—the 'crown jewels'—most critical to national security and public safety. Acting Director Nic...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Industrial Control Systems

Read Full Article →

item.title: White House Mandates US Gov Agencies to Adopt Post-Quantum Cryptography
item.description:
The White House has issued a new Executive Order to accelerate the United States' transition to post-quantum cryptography (PQC). The order, signed by President Trump on June 22, 2026, directs all federal agencies to migrate designated high-value assets to PQC standards, setting deadlines for 2030 an...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Malaysia Introduces New Cybercrime Bill to Modernize Laws, Boost Enforcement
item.description:
The Malaysian government has tabled the new Cybercrime Bill 2026 for its first reading in parliament. The bill is a significant legislative overhaul intended to replace the outdated Computer Crimes Act of 1997. It aims to provide law enforcement, particularly the National Cyber Security Agency (Nacs...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory

Read Full Article →
